rationale: The cited paper develops a household-level flow-utility model of TV behaviors (sampling/watching/ad avoidance), while the citing paper’s core contribution is a microfounded utility model of Twitch stay/join decisions and separation of direct vs. indirect channels (including advertising-related effects). This methodological and domain overlap makes a substantive use (e.g., modeling precedent/background) plausible, and with only a DOI match and no in-text snippet it is not safely classifiable as an obvious incidental mention.
